points: A linear equation in two variables is any equation that can be written in the form ax + by + c = 0, where a, b, and c are real numbers and a and b are not both zero.
Linear equations in two variables are used to represent relationships between two unknown quantities and can be formed from real-world situations or word problems.
Any linear equation in two variables can be converted to the standard form ax + by + c = 0 by rearranging terms appropriately.
A solution of a linear equation in two variables is an ordered pair (x, y) that satisfies the equation when the values are substituted into it.
Unlike linear equations in one variable which have a unique solution, linear equations in two variables have infinitely many solutions.

Solutions can be found by choosing any value for one variable and solving the resulting one-variable equation to find the corresponding value of the other variable. A quick method is to set x = 0 to find the y-value and y = 0 to find the x-value.
When all solutions of a linear equation in two variables are plotted on a Cartesian plane, they form a straight line.
Every point on the graph of a linear equation represents a solution of that equation, and conversely, every solution of the equation represents a point on its graph.
The graphical representation helps visualize the infinite solutions and the relationship between the two variables in a linear equation.
